title: NOC 10022 – Advertising, Marketing, and Public Relations Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Advertising, Marketing, and Public Relations Managers classified under NOC 10022 fall within one of the most senior and in-demand occupational groups in Canada’s immigration system. These professionals shape brand strategy, market expansion, public image, and digital presence—making them highly attractive candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).
duties: Professionals in this group may perform some or all of the following:
Direct departments that design and run advertising campaigns
Promote product and service sales across markets
Commission market-research studies and analyze results
Guide product development and pricing strategies
Manage media relations and public information programs
Represent organizations to the public and stakeholders
Plan and oversee online business platforms, intranets, and mobile applications
Coordinate digital marketing, PR, e-commerce, and social-media strategies
Across all roles, managers collaborate closely to build integrated marketing and communications strategies.
employment_requirements: Occupations in TEER 0 usually require a university degree or college diploma and several years of experience in a related field.
job_outlook: Between 2024 and 2033, Canada is expected to create 21,000 new positions for Advertising, Marketing, and Public Relations Managers, while around 22,000 job seekers may enter the field. For internationally trained professionals—particularly applicants from India—this represents promising opportunities when supported by a targeted immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Advertising, Marketing, and Public Relations Managers are in strong demand across Canada’s commercial, industrial, digital, and e-business sectors. Hiring activity accelerated after the pandemic and is expected to remain robust in the years ahead.
Excellent immigration opportunities for experienced overseas professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $125,000 to $190,262, depending on experience, location, and specialization.
Why Marketing & PR Leaders Choose Canada
Professionals immigrating under NOC 10022 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
What Is NOC 10022 (Advertising, Marketing, and Public Relations Managers)?

NOC 10022 is a senior management occupation under Canada’s National Occupational Classification (NOC 2021). It includes professionals who plan, direct, and evaluate advertising, marketing, public relations, and communications strategies designed to promote:
This occupation is categorized at TEER 0, making it highly valuable for Canadian immigration planning.
benefits: In addition to salary, many employers provide:
Employer contributions to national pension and employment-insurance programs
